articles.title: Neeraj Chopra All Set To Defend Asian Games Gold In Japan: Report
articles.description: Neeraj Chopra all set for Commonwealth Games and Asian Games, AFI chief hails his Doha Diamond League return as fantastic amidst rising javelin competition.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Senegal routs 10-man Iraq 5-0 to keep knockout hopes alive
articles.description: Senegal thrashed 10-man Iraq 5-0 in a crucial World Cup qualifier, significantly boosting their hopes of advancing. Pape Gueye netted twice after Habib Diarra opened the scoring early. Despite an early red card for Iraq's Rebin Sulaka, Senegal capitalized, with Ismaila Sarr and Iliman Ndiaye also finding the net. Now with three points and a strong goal difference, Senegal awaits other results to determine their fate in the knockout stages.

articles.title: Winning FIFA World Cup group takes a backseat to getting ready for the knockout stage for some teams
articles.description: FFIA World Cup 2026: Coaches at the football tournament are shifting strategies, prioritizing player rest and injury prevention over group stage dominance. Norway, without star Haaland, lost to France but focused on a longer tournament run. Portugal's Martinez aims for wins, while Colombia's Lorenzo considers opponent matchups. Spain's de la Fuente emphasizes winning every game to prepare for eventual tough opponents.

articles.title: Pakistan vs Netherlands, West Indies vs Ireland, England vs New Zealand Women's T20 World Cup match today: Predicted playing XI, head-to-head, pitch report, weather update
articles.description: The ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026 group stage reaches its climax with three decisive matches. England has already secured a semifinal spot, while West Indies are in a strong position. New Zealand's fate depends on other results, and Pakistan and the Netherlands aim to avoid a winless tournament. Pitch reports from Bristol and London suggest a preference for chasing, with dew being a factor at The Oval.

articles.title: Phil Mickelson allegedly forced out of two luxury golf clubs amid repeated personal conduct allegations, report says
articles.description: Phil Mickelson's conduct controversies have grown beyond the initial allegations at The Farms Golf Club. A new report by Alan Shipnuck places his removal within a broader pattern, citing expulsions from two additional private clubs, the Madison Club and The Bridges in Rancho Santa Fe, on grounds of personal conduct. An unrelated 2015 account from Ashley Perez, former wife of golfer Pat Perez, has also emerged publicly. Mickelson has not personally addressed any of the claims, with his legal team maintaining that all allegations are false while he remains with his family during a private health matter.

articles.description: Scotland, playing only its second Women’s T20 World Cup, was aiming to finish fourth in the group and claim direct qualification for the 2028 edition in Pakistan.

articles.title: Spain vs Uruguay: Who is Alex Baena? Midfielder's goal sends Spain into World Cup knockout stage
articles.description: Spain defeated Uruguay 1-0 to finish top of Group H and qualify for the FIFA World Cup knockout stage, with Alex Baena scoring the decisive goal. The 24-year-old Atletico Madrid winger netted his first World Cup goal, continuing his rise after spells with Villarreal and Girona. The defeat knocked Uruguay out of the tournament.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup: Reece James out for England's match against Panama
articles.description: Thomas Tuchel has confirmed that Reece James will miss England's final World Cup group match against Panama due to a hamstring injury. However, Bukayo Saka, Declan Rice, and Elliot Anderson are available for the crucial qualifier.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup: Record-breaker Lionel Messi rested for Argentina's group finale against Jordan
articles.description: To manage Lionel Messi's workload, Argentina's coach Lionel Scaloni has decided to bench the captain for the last Group J match against Jordan. This tactical choice comes after their team has already clinched the top position, focusing instead on keeping Messi fit for the intense knockout stages, especially following his notable injury and outstanding performances.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Day 16 in pictures: Best fan moments from France, Spain, Cabo Verde and Belgium matches
articles.description: Day 16 of the FIFA World Cup 2026 saw France, Spain, and Belgium top their groups, with Cabo Verde and Senegal advancing. While stars like Mbappe and Haaland were in action, Dembele's brilliance led France to a 4-1 win. Uruguay's journey ended, while Iran awaits their fate after a crucial draw. The day was filled with thrilling football and passionate fan moments.

articles.title: Cabo Verde reach knockouts in historic World Cup debut, book Round of 32 showdown with Messi’s Argentina
articles.description: Cabo Verde has etched its name in World Cup history, reaching the knockout stage in its debut tournament as the smallest nation to do so. The "Blue Sharks" showcased remarkable resilience, drawing all three group matches, including against Spain and Uruguay, to advance without a win. This historic feat sets up a thrilling Round of 32 clash against tournament favorites Argentina, marking a significant moment for African football.

articles.title: What is Toronto Maple Leafs head coach Jim Hiller’s theory on Auston Matthews production dip?
articles.description: New Toronto Maple Leafs head coach Jim Hiller believes Auston Matthews' recent goal production dip isn't due to individual decline, but rather the team's struggling power play. Hiller, who previously coached Matthews as an assistant, aims to revitalize the special teams, hoping it will positively impact the star center's five-on-five performance. Matthews is also recovering from injuries and is expected to return soon.
